notes: Constructor declarations use def! name(...). The name new is the default construction entrypoint.

A declaration binds one nominal non-value capability identity to one normalized nonempty EffectRow and ends at StatementBoundary. It performs no effect, grants no authority, creates no value or conformance, and remains disjoint from the callable's explicit effects row and context permission channel.
Inside an extension-set body the enclosing declaration supplies the extension domain: write +def selector, never +def selector.
Named functions admit a block, explicit `= return Expr` shorthand, or declarative clause body. Bare `= Expr` is not current. A terminal valueless return is canonically omitted.
Every normal non-Unit value path uses explicit `return expr`. Unit functions complete canonically by fallthrough; an unguarded terminal bare return is valid but noncanonical.

Declaration named-rest parameters and function-type named-rest residues use attached `***`. Call and materialization named unfold alone use attached prefix `**`. A collector is final, unique, and exactly the canonical Prelude Record carrier. Parameter/type `**` is removed and no gate or compatibility mode admits it.

display_name: Named static constraint predicate checker-internal law
notes: Checker-owned named predicates such as HasFiniteStaticShape<T>; arbitrary user CTFE constraints are not current source. Current contract: Checker-owned predicate names are internal design, not user CTFE.
Current contract aligns negative fixture definition, rejected smoke corpus, runner output, and parser receipt seed counts. Runner execution remains NOT_RUN.

General negative impl remains design-only outside compiler-known closed-world facts. Current contract deliberately retains this in preview/design/profile because authority, ABI, memory model, or coherence risk is not closed.

Nested //-...-// comments use a nesting-depth stack. Any positive dash-run closer closes exactly the innermost open comment; opener and closer dash counts need not match. Matching counts are recommended style only and a mismatch may produce a non-blocking warning.

display_name: Nested local function minimum sound profile
notes: A block-local def/def#pure/def#async/def#guard is a BlockItem, has lexical visibility after declaration, carries no visibility modifier, and lists every outer capture explicitly in CaptureList.
Class inheritance never manufactures Trait conformance. A descendant declares conformance explicitly or names an explicit `via` delegation/evidence path.
Stable: assignment/call conversion between String, Char, and Bytes is explicit; interpolation rendering is a Display operation, not conversion.

`base!{...}` and `base!!{...}` construct a fresh value of exactly base's nominal type through that type's visible ConstructionRow. Labels are checked once; `!` preserves field responsibilities shallowly, while `!!` deep-clones only fields whose declared responsibility admits deep clone. Neither form changes nominal type or creates structural conformance.
Non-current trace identity retained for reference integrity; it has no source activation or independent semantics.
Current law: No-parameter lambdas may omit `=>` only when an expected function context fixes arity 0; empty `{}` needs expected `() -> Unit`.
Underdefined inherited design seed retained for redesign only. R51b grants no source activation until a normative semantic rule, diagnostic, and positive/negative trace are authored.
downgrade_reason: no substantive predecessor rule and no diagnostic/predicate/example trace

notes: Current trace-bounded responsibility for NumericArray context anchor core law. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.
Current law: `&matrix + row` marks matrix as context-provider anchor for the nearest eligible NumericArray operation.

display_name: Stable polarity law for NumericArray context anchors
notes: Current trace-bounded responsibility for Stable polarity law for NumericArray context anchors. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.
Current trace-bounded responsibility for NumericArray context-anchor semantic core. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.
Current trace-bounded responsibility for NumericArray elementwise arithmetic MSP. No authority, ownership, effect, witness, or source activation is implied beyond the exact current references.
Current contract: NumericArray infix `A ^ n` elementwise power is Preview-gated. Stable `A^` is postfix transpose.
Non-current trace identity retained for reference integrity; it has no source activation or independent semantics.
Current contract: NumericArray LSP responsibility card. This row grants no hidden source activation, authority, or product implementation claim beyond the stated status and companion rules.

NumericArray owns a distinct typed-axis domain; every built-in default source-visible coordinate is 1..dimension. A multi-axis slice yields a provenance-retaining readonly view, never an implicit copy, rebase, mutable assignment, lifetime escape, or isolation transfer.

An attached CaretToken is owned only by NumericArrayTransposeSuffix on the postfix route. Its semantic result is a nonowning owner-bounded readonly coordinate view: rank two swaps axes, rank one flips an admitted orientation witness, and an unoriented rank-one operand rejects. The law grants no implicit element copy, language-observable allocation, mutation, lifetime escape, isolation crossing, adjoint, or shareability inference.
The inferred column-vector semicolon form is owned by ShapeInferredColumnVectorLiteral.
`#[...]` is owned by NumericArrayShapeInferredLiteral and does not use the exact-shape opener token.

display_name: NumericArray sharp-shape and shape-inferred value literal MSP
notes: Sharp-shape literal/type heads use DecimalIntegerToken dimensions and SharpShapeOpenToken; inferred and exact initializers have the listed disjoint routes.
Current Stable semantic law: rank-1 NumericArray orientation witness is part of the shape descriptor.

Numeric tokens exclude sign. NumericLiteralAdmitted returns an exact value or unsuffixed-integer magnitude fact. With an exact integer target, the checker may adapt only direct PrefixExpr(-, literal) by testing -magnitude; no other fold or hidden conversion. IntN/UIntN use N={8,16,32,64,128}; ISize/USize need suffix/conversion; Float defaults Float64 and Float32 needs f32. Product NOT_RUN.
Integer and float suffixes are closed lexical terminals and map through the current numeric type contract.

NumericLiteralAdmitted owns normalization. Scalar +,-,*,/ require one exact Int/Float domain; % is integer-only and /,% truncate quotient toward zero. Intrinsic-reserved pairs remain intrinsic. Exact binary +,-,* may use the separately bounded Stable Add/Subtract/Multiply conformance profile for a non-intrinsic pair. Integer ^ needs one exact domain plus static nonnegative proof. Measure/shape/pointwise use owner laws. ArithmeticDefect precedes commit; there is no hidden conversion, result-directed selection, fallback, or runtime relookup.
